body.title: Stale /compact request fires with wrong session ID
body.result: 10min mtime staleness check in _check_compact_pending drops abandoned pending files without firing the reminder. New test test_drops_stale_file covers it. Commit 5ab111be.
body.description:
Mid-sprint on 2026-04-18 (session 2026-04-18b), a UserPromptSubmit hook reminder fired:

```
--- COMPACT REQUESTED ---
A /compact was requested by the prior turn but couldn't execute mid-response.
Run /compact now with this preserve string:
preserve: session=2026-04-16a
--- end compact request ---
```

## Problem
- Preserve string references session 2026-04-16a — TWO DAYS OLD, from a different session
- Active session was 2026-04-18b
- No /compact was requested in the active session
- Looks like leaked state from a hook that persists compact-request markers across sessions without scoping them

## Impact
- Misleading reminder. Ignored in this instance, but if CC trusted it and ran /compact with a stale preserve string, the wrong state would be retained
- Suggests a persistent flag or file that was written during 2026-04-16a and never cleared

## First steps
- Find what writes the COMPACT REQUESTED marker. Likely one of the hooks in ~/.claude/settings.json — probably cc_hook_pending.py at /home/akien/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_hook_pending.py
- Check if the marker file is session-scoped or global
- Make sure it gets cleared at /savestate or session start

## Workaround
- Ignore compact reminders that reference a different session than your current one
